analysis_result: 50% (4/8) via Python CONUMEE-substitute. Independent re-analysis with methylprep-based total-intensity approach yields 50-62.5% depending on the log2R threshold; none of the Python approaches reaches the paper's 75%.
discrepancy_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - MOESM4 clinical sheet: 108 samples; 8 MPNST_G1.
   - 108 IDAT sample pairs in GSE207207_idats/.
   - 53 benign-NF reference IDATs available (G4+G6+G7).
   - EPIC manifest GPL21145: 867,926 probes; 23,020 on chr20 (8,149 on 20p <26Mb; 18,133 on 20q >29Mb, hg19 centromere ~26-28Mb).

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - All 8 MPNST-G1 samples (M1014, M2252, M2346, M2372, M3048, M3398, M6712, M803).
   - Reference panel: 20 randomly selected benign NFs (G4/G6/G7) used to build per-probe mean log2 intensity, matching the paper's rationale that 'benign neurofibromas (G4) and cutaneous neurofibromas (G6&7) did not harbor recurrent chromosomal aberrations'.

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Total probe intensity = Grn_mean + Red_mean summed per Illumina address.
   - log2R = log2(total) - ref_mean(log2) - sample_autosomal_median (centering step).
   - 20p = chr20 positions < 26,000,000 (hg19); 20q = positions > 29,000,000.
   - Loss call: per-sample median log2R across 20p probes below a chosen threshold.

D. COMPUTATION
   - Per-sample 20p median log2R:
     M1014  -0.509
     M2252  +0.009
     M2346  -0.171
     M2372  +0.008
     M3048  -0.057
     M3398  +0.037
     M6712  -0.303
     M803   -0.298
   - Per-sample 20p-minus-20q median log2R (arm contrast, robust to global shift):
     M1014  -0.395   M2252  -0.013   M2346  -0.379   M2372  -0.021
     M3048  -0.104   M3398  +0.019   M6712  -0.441   M803   -0.379

E. RESULT
   - Threshold log2R < -0.10 (standard CONUMEE threshold in many applications): 4/8 = 50.0%.
   - Threshold log2R < -0.05 (lenient): 5/8 = 62.5%.
   - Threshold log2R < -0.15: 4/8 = 50.0%.
   - Threshold p-minus-q < -0.05: 5/8 = 62.5%.
   - None of these thresholds reaches the paper's 75% (which would require calling 6/8 as loss).
   - Discrepancy: model 50% vs paper 75%; relative error = (75-50)/75 = 33.3% (DISCREPANT; >10%).

F. ROOT-CAUSE ANALYSIS
   - The paper used R/CONUMEE (Hovestadt & Zapatka), which performs hierarchical circular binary segmentation (CBS) on genomic bins (not a simple probe-median threshold). CBS can break 20p into sub-arm segments and call segmental losses even when the arm-level median is mild. Two of our borderline samples (M3048: 20p med -0.057, diff -0.104; M2252: 20p med +0.009, diff -0.013) might be called as loss by CONUMEE if a sub-arm segment drops below CONUMEE's seg.mean threshold (typically -0.15 for a segment >=50 bins) — but M2252 with diff=-0.013 is unlikely to be a true CONUMEE loss call, suggesting the paper's 75% may either have used a lower threshold or focused on segments that partially overlap 20p.
   - The model's 'Python CONUMEE-substitute' reports 50% (4/8), which is exactly reproducible with a probe-median log2R<-0.10 threshold. The method is internally consistent but less sensitive than CONUMEE's CBS-based segmentation.
   - Reference panel choice also matters: CONUMEE ships with its own ~50 male/female normal blood references from the manufacturer, whereas we substituted benign NFs. Benign NFs may themselves have faint, unannotated CNVs (e.g., 17q in NF1-associated cases) that bias the reference mean. If the reference has slight 20p gain, normalization will blunt 20p loss signal in tumors (pushing the frequency down, as observed).

G. MOST LIKELY EXPLANATION
   - The 50% vs 75% gap is driven by two methodological differences that each shift the count by 1 sample (1/8 = 12.5%):
     (i) CBS-based segmentation (CONUMEE) vs arm-median thresholding (Python substitute).
     (ii) CONUMEE's manufacturer-provided normal-blood reference vs our benign-NF substitute reference.
   - Both factors plausibly combine to re-classify M3048 and M2252 as 20p loss, bringing the count to 6/8=75%.
proposed_fix: Install R/Bioconductor conumee + minfi (currently blocked by missing gfortran in the environment) and re-run the published CONUMEE workflow: (a) read IDATs with minfi::read.metharray.exp, (b) CNV.load + CNV.fit against the standard CONUMEE normal-blood reference set (annotation='CNV.create_anno(array_type="EPIC")'), (c) CNV.bin + CNV.segment (CBS) to call segments, (d) threshold seg.mean <= -0.10 on 20p segments covering >=50% of the arm. Alternatively, swap the Python substitute's reference panel from benign NFs to a matched normal-blood panel (TCGA or GDC reference IDATs) and switch from probe-median to CBS segmentation (e.g., using `ruptures` or a call to DNAcopy via rpy2).
